review_title: Accurate tracking of time in attendance, vacation, sick, and personal time.
description: I often work alone in the sales office, and my attendance is verified by the TimeClock Plus punches throughout the day. Very accurate reporting of time in attendance, when working without on-site supervision.
author: Tiffany T.
pros: I like the self-service feature of TimeClock Plus. All clocked hours are visible over the course of the pay period. Requests for punch adjustments are submitted through the software, as well as requests for use of vacation time, and reporting of sick time used. The software is linked to the workstation IP address, so punches can only be made while in attendance.
cons: Occassionally time punches are not logged the first click through. I always double check the daily log to ensure each punch is recorded, and sometimes have to click through a second time. If I get rushed and a punch isn't recorded, the approving manager is able to make adjustments.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: I like how simple it is to clock in and out with the software
cons: Wish there were more features and options with the software

review_title: TimeClock Plus - good for a small office
description: TimeClock Plus was easy to set up and use, and we rarely had to call customers service, we could fix many problems on our own, which made things much easier for our office.
author: Dawn L.
pros: TimeClock Plus was a great value for the price, and it was easy for employees to use. It was also easy for us to track hours to enter into our payroll software.
cons: We quickly outgrew TimeClock Plus and it could not support our growing practice on its own. We had to purchase another software that could function as a timeclock, as well as process payroll to simplify our process.

review_title: TimeClock Plus customer review experience - Completed on 06/05/2025
description: Overall, my experience has been very positive when working with TimeClock Plus. The technical support staff has always assisted me well and provided excellent support.
author: Ed S.
pros: Good software experience and excellent professionalism working with customer and technical support. They've always been very patient with me and worked with me to resolve any of my software upgrade needs.
cons: Sometimes, I've had to wait a bit of time for a response back when calling or emailing in regarding technical support.
author_profession: V.P. - Business Management, I.T. Administration, and Finance

description: Overall, my experience has been very good. Each time I come up against something that is not working, the support team has been able to offer a fix. They are very knowledgeable and patient with what the situation /need is.
author: Doris Z.
pros: What I like most about TCP is that it meets and in, some cases, exceeds my needs & expectations. The program works great for the flexible shifts, employee information, reporting, etc.
cons: The only thing I do not like about TCP is that I cannot drag and drop shifts in the advanced scheduler.

description: Overall I think TimeClock Plus is the most versatile time clock system. I have used a few others and I would choose this one.
author: Bailie M.
pros: I liked how secure it was. The code to get in is just the right length and I don't feel like employees can guess other employees codes.
cons: At times as the manager it does take a while to enter missed punches. I think an option that the employee can enter their missed punch and then I can review would be better.

description: Overall, I really enjoy Timeclock Plus. I keep track of all vacation hours here & I can upload and download hours and bring them into QuickBooks easily. That alone cuts my time for payroll in half.
author: Candace G.
pros: I like how easy it is to get help when you need it. The techs are very knowledgeable and nice. I try to search the knowledge base first, but if I am unsure I get a ticket going and the techs are quick to help me solve the issue.
cons: It can be a little overwhelming to get everything set up just right. Some settings are strange or not clear. It's a powerful software, but that comes with a learning curve.

description: We are very happy with TCP. It is easy to use for the employee and for those who set up employees or run reports.
author: Karen E.
pros: I like the flexibility of the reports. The ability to filter by a variety of functions is helpful to me when I need to see only specific employees or job codes.
cons: One of the reports I run is not currently able to be separated by employee. I believe the tech support folks are working on that!

description: see above- I feel like this is a redundant question. Now I have to type fifty more letters to fill the requirement
author: Misty A.
pros: I like that if I have an issue or question, I can call and get excellent help no matter the problem.
cons: I feel like the cost is a little high but isn't everything? We have used TCP for years and plan on being customers for a long time.

description: I have had overall positive experiencing with TimeClock Plus! It is so easy to use and we have many employees who use this as their way of clocking hours. I love it.
author: Shannon M.
pros: It is super easy! It is so straight forward, there is really hardly any training, and quick to get used to!
cons: Sometimes we have error codes that will populate. But I feel like that may be an issue on our end.

review_title: Simple and easy to use platform for staff time keeping
description: Generally positive, with ongoing maintenance through our IT department. Front end user interface for employees and managers is simple and easy to use, with little upfront training required.
author: Edward M.
pros: Allows for a streamlined integration with our financial software and digital timeclock processes.
cons: Outdated user interface and reporting features, with some limited customer support options.

description: Overall experience has been great, it’s very easy to use and no one has had any trouble using.
author: Glenn B.
pros: Very easy to use, no member of staff has ever had difficulty
cons: No real concerns. Sometimes freezes but I think that is more an issue with us, than the product

description: It has been very pleasing to use. Easier to understand.
author: Eric W.
pros: Easy access to setup. Employees have no problem using it.
cons: None that I can think about. It’s been very good.

review_title: Improved communication for both employer and employee
description: Timeclock plus has helped to improve communication between management and employees. Multiple paper forms that had to go through the correct chain of command to get to the right person have been removed as a result. Employees can go to one place to enter a request or correction and we can determine everyone who should see it and that they are properly notified. Frustration is largely reduced and we have been able to pass out certain tasks to lower level personnel because access levels restrict who can or cannot see sensitive information. Overall we have been very please with TimeClock Plus and the service we receive.
author: Mike S.
pros: Ability for employees to connect remotely to check hours or request time off. Export features that speed up the work of payroll. Audit capabilities that track who made changes and when. Automated reports that send information to the right people at the right time. Web-based platform that allows for multiple clock-in points to reduce congestion around timeclock at sign-in and lunch time. Responsive Tech support reps that are well versed in the product and able to answer questions or explain features clearly. Ability to send a message from one place to every active employee for emergency notifications or updates.
cons: Extensive features that, while great, can be a bit confusing to navigate or find depending on how they are labeled.

author: Elizabeth C.
pros: Wasn't much that I liked about this software, a lot of time wasted.
cons: Not user friendly, had to call customer service constantly for help.

description: I found the system one of the most difficult of the 68 Software Systems our IT Department supports. The granularity of the Application is extremely complex. The Context Help is well intentioned, but almost impossible to follow, as the multi-instruction Help Bubbles disappear based on Cursor Context. I have had to hover over the Help content I am seeking, use Greenshot to capture a screen grab, then Paste the content into a Word document, repeating the process until I have what I need. Terminology is also confusing.
author: Greg B.
pros: As an Administrator of the User side of the TC Plus application, I find that the "Copy" function of User Assignments is EXTREMELY helpful. There are perhaps Hundreds of Property Settings that may be attributed to a User, particularly Supervisors & Managers. When I add a Supervisor to an OU (Organizational Unit), I find the "Copy " Profile Assignments from a like Supervisor to be a valuable time-saving function.
cons: The nesting of various functions is non-intuitive. I recall the first time I attempted to change a Users Password, the difficulty in locating the Form Controls which managed this method were extremely confusion. Also, the "Search" method is a bit confusion, and is not Inherited by all forms as one would expect. Once on an Employee, clicking "Roles", for example, does not display the Roles the Employee has, but defaults to the Role settings within the Organization.

review_title: Clock in and out with ease
author: Kimberly V.
pros: TimeClock is perfect for people that need an accurate way to look at all of their working hours and clock in an out with ease. It is a perfect software to use with employees and it is so easy to do. When we first implemented the software in my school district it took no time for employees to get adjusted and understand what they needed to do. I have already recommeded this software to others
cons: I liked everything about TimeClock Plus no complains!

review_title: The best time-tracking software put to the web in some time.
description: All in all, TimeClock Plus was a splendid experience as a non-exempt employee. Making it simple to do the regular tasks, as well as keep track of all logged time and earned time has made it my favorite application ever for clocking employee time.
author: Curry M.
pros: This is one of those rare cases where an entire review could be summed up in just the pros. To start, it's never been simpler to track your time, request leave, and do it all remotely at the same time before TCP. When you want to view time logged, you just go to view and select the option, and it's there! The design is simplistic and pleasant to look at. The absolute coolest feature is also being able to access earned leave time on the web and remotely. This is a fantastic addition, and on top of it you can do projected earned time up to 20+ years from the current date. This may be a small thing for some, but it's a huge deal when you want to know how much time you're being rewarded with when working 40+ hours a week.
cons: One of the only constant annoyances was a two-step login that could be tedious when forgetting to clock in, or forgetting to clock out.

description: I clock in daily using this software. It is a simple, easy software to use. I would recommend any business to use this software.
author: Chris H.
pros: I am an educator and we use Time Clock Plus daily to clock in for work. It is simple and user-friendly. It is also easy to access the dashboard and see your current hours.
cons: The downside is that employers can put controls on there as you won't be able to clock in with the phone app because your district doesn't allow it.

author: Verified Reviewer
pros: It is very user friendly, easy to train new users.
cons: When you have a large group, looking at Group Hours is very tedious. We have about 1500 users and it takes so long to load the group hours for the whole company. I have to break it down by school and do one at a time to save time. If you do bring everyone up, going from page to page is very slow.

description: It is a good little piece of equipment. It does what it needs to do, even if it has a couple of quirks at the moment. I think the function to view leave requests is great, though I would prefer it not auto pull them up once you attempt to clock in.
author: Angela B.
pros: - Easy to clock in and out. Most of the time. - Ability to see leave request approval/declines is pretty nice. - Sturdy piece of equipment.
cons: - Sometimes it lags when you're attempting to clock in for the day. - It has a few bug that should be worked out, such as attempting to punch in too fast after a previous person punches out. I think overtime those will be ironed out.

description: Terrible and incomplete implementation process. I am hard pressed to think of one thing that has worked as we were told it would.
author: Brenda L.
pros: Time tracking is good, but there are better out there. Mostly chose for scheduling system that was sold as being very specific to our industry.
cons: Too many to list. It is not at all what we were "sold". Implementation is a incomplete nightmare. The implementation "specialists" dont even know how the software works. They are just "data inputter's" and they do very little of that even. We've had the system for well over a year and it is still not set up and has many "technical" issues.

description: My employer uses TimeClock Plus to keep track of everyone in our business. It is a simple way for us to request leave, enter time, and keep track of leave accrued. We went to TimeClock Plus from printed, handwritten spreadsheets. TCP has been a lifesaver!
author: Verified Reviewer
pros: TimeClock Plus makes it super simple for us to enter our time worked as well as keep track of leave earned. It is incredibly user-friendly and I love using it every day.
cons: Working mainly from an employee standpoint (rather than a manger or an admin), I have no issues with the program.

description: My overall experience with TimeClock Plus has been awesome. My staff members can easily log their hours, request time off, and even track the type of leave they are requesting (i.e. personal, vacation, etc).
author: Monique B.
pros: TimeClock Plus is beyond easy to use. The system automatically calculates your time, deducts lunch breaks, and even allows superiors and subordinates to communicate via time approval/denial messages.
cons: Sometimes the time clock requests for sick, vacation, personal days, etc. are sent to the head administrator's spam folder, which can leave the employee wondering if their request was submitted correctly. Employees cannot clock in or out when off the company's network. This becomes a pain when staff members are traveling for work.

description: The software itself works DECENT but again the customer service, lack of effort or personalization you receive is thee worst I've experience working with vendor. I would take your business elsewhere to a company that will give you the time of day. Especially for a $1200+ implementation fee
author: Sarah C.
pros: I strictly use it on a mobile app for my employees to clock in and out. I like it because I was told it could geo-map so employees can only use the app when in the building. They forgot the fine print: it is extremely unreliable with location recognition, the geomap is a circle so it has to include some of the outside of the building.
cons: $1200 for an implementation fee that could have been a 5-minute video, my account "implementation specialist" did not set my accounts up per my parameters (something the $1200 fee is supposed to include). I was not properly explained how licensing was charged, invoiced, given no explanation of charges when you ask and the money was withdrawn from my account. When you call to voice a terrible experience you are pretty much told too bad and they do nothing to make it right for the client.
author_duration: Used the software for: Less than 6 months
